items.title: Liverpool-uitblinker geprezen door Gerrard: 'Doet me telkens denken aan Torres'
items.summary: Steven Gerrard is overtuigd van de kwaliteiten van Hugo Ekitiké. De Franse spits was zaterdag belangrijk voor Liverpool door twee goals te maken tegen Newcastle United . Gerrard vindt Ekitiké wel wat weg hebben van Fernando Torres.

items.title: VI Live: PSV kan Feyenoord mokerslag uitdelen in titelstrijd
items.summary: Zondagmiddag om 14.30 uur staat de topper tussen PSV en Feyenoord op het programma. PSV hoopt de concurrent een mokerslag uit te kunnen delen in de titelstrijd, terwijl de Rotterdammers de marge kunnen terugbrengen naar elf punten. Volg de wedstrijd op de voet in de VI Live.

items.title: Feestelijke avond Salah met Liverpool: aanvaller breekt twee records
items.summary: Met zijn assist voor Liverpool tegen Newcastle United bereikte Mohamed Salah twee nieuwe mijlpalen. Hij troefde Thierry Henry en Wayne Rooney af als productiefste speler op eigen veld én werd de eerste voetballer in de geschiedenis van de Premier League die tegen dezelfde tegenstander minimaal tien goals en assists noteerde.

items.title: De liefde tussen profclub Málaga en een Nederlandse Vijfdeklasser
items.summary: In de rubriek De Basis gaat Voetbal International op zoek naar bijzondere verhalen in het amateurvoetbal. Deze week over de liefde tussen de Nederlandse Vijfdeklasser VV Melissant en de voormalige Spaanse hoogvlieger Málaga.

items.title: Daan en Mats Rots over de felle kritiek: 'Mijn lessen kan ik hem meegeven'
items.summary: Daan Rots (24) ging bij FC Twente de laatste jaren vaak gebukt onder de felle kritiek van de eigen achterban, maar de rechtsbuiten heeft geleerd daarmee om te gaan. Hij vertelt erover in een dubbelinterview met teamgenoot en broertje Mats (19).

items.title: Liverpool 'all there for' Konaté after father's de...
items.summary: Arne Slot has hailed the professionalism of defender Ibrahima Konaté after he scored for Liverpool on his return to the team following the death of his father.

items.title: 0-2 at HT, 3-2 at FT! Chelsea in epic comeback win...
items.summary: Chelsea came from two goals down to seal a remarkable 3-2 victory over relegation-threatened West Ham United on Saturday.

items.title: Arsenal thrash Leeds to reinstate 7-point PL lead
items.summary: Arsenal have reinstated their seven-point lead at the top of the Premier League after a 4-0 win against Leeds United.

items.title: Once laughed at, Liam Rosenior is earning Chelsea ...
items.summary: Despite the critics who came following his appointment, Chelsea coach Liam Rosenior continues to prove them wrong.

items.title: Hugo Ekitike's brilliance gets Liverpool's season ...
items.summary: Liverpool's title defence might be over but, in Hugo Ekitike, the Reds have a striker that is the envy of all their rivals -- particularly Newcastle.

items.title: Rose leads by SIX at Farmers Insurance Open with record score in sight
items.summary: Justin Rose is six shots clear after the third round of the Farmers Insurance Open at Torrey Pines with a record tournament score and 13th PGA Tour title in his sights.

items.title: Kelly recovers from knockdown to become latest British world champion
items.summary: Britain's Josh Kelly outpointed Bakhram Murtazaliev to win the IBF light-middleweight title in Newcastle.

items.title: Rashford and Yamal on target in Barcelona win over Elche
Ekitike double helps Liverpool cruise past Newcastle
items.summary: Summer signing Huge Ekitike scores twice as Liverpool record their first league win of 2026, putting four goals past Newcastle United.

items.title: Chelsea fight back to beat West Ham in dramatic derby
items.summary: Chelsea come back from being two goals down at half-time to beat West Ham in stoppage time.

items.title: Kroupi scores stunner as Bournemouth beat Wolves
items.summary: 19-year-old Eli Juniour Kroupi scores his eight Premier League goal of the season to help Bournemouth beat Wolves 2-0.

items.title: Everton's Beto nets stoppage-time leveller at Brighton
items.summary: Beto celebrates his birthday by scoring in stoppage time for Everton to salvage a point in a hard-fought draw against Brighton.

items.title: Arsenal score four in superb win against Leeds
items.summary: Arsenal get their title charge back on track with a dominant display against Leeds, their first win in four Premier League games.

items.title: Pep Lijnders reveals Klopp persuaded him to take Manchester City assistant role
items.summary: Pep Guardiola’s No 2 sought Klopp’s blessing for role Lijnders: ‘Jurgen said he would take the job if I didn’t’ Pep Lijnders has revealed Jürgen Klopp convinced him to take the job as Pep Guardiola’s assistant at Manchester City, despite the German spending almost a decade at their fierce rivals Liverpool. He said Klopp had joked about taking the role if Lijnders didn’t. Lijnders joined Guardiola’s coaching staff in the summer and will be alongside the Spaniard, who missed Friday’s press conference for “personal reasons”, at Tottenham on Sunday. Lijnders spent almost 10 years at Liverpool, mostly alongside Klopp, and the pair had three title battles with City, twice finishing as runners-up. That sparked some internal debate for Lijnders when the opportunity arose to move to Manchester. Continue reading...

items.title: Ekitiké and Wirtz sink Newcastle before Konaté seals welcome Liverpool win
items.summary: You could say Hugo Ekitiké was the one who got away from Newcastle last summer, except there were so many. But with a certain inevitability and a devastating, gamechanging performance, the Liverpool striker haunted Eddie Howe and the club who coveted him all the same. Pain came in various forms for Newcastle. Superior for 40 minutes and deservedly ahead through Anthony Gordon, the visitors trailed at the interval thanks to a quick-fire brace from the player they tried to sign from Eintracht Frankfurt. Liverpool swept in for Ekitiké instead and their initial £69m outlay, rising to £79m, is proving money very well spent. Continue reading...
